questions.text: Why does the Surah begin with urgent commands like 'arise and warn'?
questions.answer: Because the message is serious and time is short; guidance must be delivered clearly.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that da'wah should be delayed until conditions are comfortable.
questions.answer: False. It commands immediate warning and action.

questions.text: What does 'magnify your Lord' teach about the heart’s focus in da'wah?
questions.answer: Keep Allah central—His greatness, not people’s reactions or status.

questions.text: How does Surah Al-Muddaththir connect outward purity and inward purity?
questions.answer: It calls to purification and also to avoiding idolatry and spiritual filth.

questions.text: What is one practical meaning of avoiding 'rujz' today?
questions.answer: Avoid shirk and sinful impurities—anything that corrupts worship and character.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about patience in the face of rejection?
questions.answer: Remain patient for Allah’s sake; do not let rejection break your mission or sincerity.

questions.text: Why is the Hereafter introduced early through the image of the Trumpet?
questions.answer: To make accountability vivid and urgent, so people do not live heedlessly.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah suggests that denying the Hereafter is a harmless opinion.
questions.answer: False. It presents denial as a path to severe punishment.

questions.text: What does the story of the wealthy denier teach about arrogance toward revelation?
questions.answer: Wealth and influence can fuel pride, leading people to label truth as false to protect ego.

questions.text: How does the Surah expose the psychology of someone who calls revelation 'magic'?
questions.answer: It shows calculated thinking and plotting, not honest confusion.

questions.text: What does Saqar represent in the Surah’s warning?
questions.answer: A severe Hellfire punishment that exposes worldly pride as worthless.

questions.text: Why does the Surah mention a specific number of angels guarding Hell?
questions.answer: As a test for deniers and a confirmation for sincere believers, showing the unseen is real.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the details of the unseen are meant for mockery.
questions.answer: False. Mockery is condemned; details are meant as warning and proof.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about personal responsibility for deeds?
questions.answer: Each soul is held for what it earns; excuses do not remove accountability.

questions.text: What does Surah Al-Muddaththir teach about the importance of prayer?
questions.answer: Neglecting prayer is listed among major reasons criminals enter punishment.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about feeding the poor as part of faith?
questions.answer: Neglecting the poor is a serious moral failure tied to punishment.

questions.text: How does the Surah connect social morality with belief in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: Those who deny the Hereafter often neglect prayer, charity, and fall into vain talk.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that vain talk is spiritually neutral.
questions.answer: False. It lists vain talk as part of the path to punishment.

questions.text: What is one practical way to reduce vain talk in daily life?
questions.answer: Choose purposeful speech: remembrance, learning, honest work, and avoiding gossip.

questions.text: Why does the Surah say intercession will not help the criminals?
questions.answer: Because they destroyed their faith through major neglect and denial; they come with no sincere foundation.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about people fleeing the reminder like frightened donkeys?
questions.answer: It exposes irrational fear of guidance and refusal to listen.

questions.text: How can a believer avoid becoming someone who flees reminders?
questions.answer: Keep a humble heart, attend reminders, and act on small teachings consistently.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about demanding personal special proof (pages for me)?
questions.answer: It shows arrogance—people want customized proof instead of submitting to clear guidance.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that the Quran is only for a specific group.
questions.answer: False. It is a reminder, but only those Allah wills truly benefit by remembering.

questions.text: What does 'worthy of fear and worthy of forgiveness' teach about Allah?
questions.answer: Allah deserves awe and obedience, and He also forgives those who repent sincerely.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Muddaththir' mean?
questions.answer: The Cloaked One or The Covered One (another address to the Prophet SAW).

questions.text: What are the first three commands given to the Prophet in this Surah?
questions.answer: Arise and warn, magnify your Lord, and purify your clothing.

questions.text: What should the Prophet avoid according to this Surah?
questions.answer: 'Ar-Rujz' (idolatry or uncleanness).

questions.text: What is the 'Trumpet' called in this Surah?
questions.answer: The 'Naqur'.

questions.text: Describe the type of man mentioned who was wealthy but rejected the Quran after thinking and plotting.
questions.answer: A wealthy and powerful man who was given much, but when he heard the Quran, he plotted and said it was 'nothing but magic'.

questions.text: What will Allah throw this man into?
questions.answer: Saqar (a level of Hellfire).

questions.text: How is 'Saqar' described?
questions.answer: It leaves nothing and spares nothing, and it blackens/scorches the skin.

questions.text: How many angels are in charge of Saqar?
questions.answer: Nineteen.

questions.text: Why did Allah specify the number nineteen for the angels of Hell?
questions.answer: As a trial for the disbelievers, to convince the people of the Book, and to increase the believers in faith.

questions.text: What is described as 'one of the greatest signs' in this Surah?
questions.answer: Saqar (Hellfire).

questions.text: Is every soul a pledge for its own deeds?
questions.answer: Yes, 'Every soul, for what it has earned, will be retained'.

questions.text: Who are the exception to being 'retained' for their deeds?
questions.answer: The companions of the right (Ashab al-Yamin).

questions.text: What four reasons will the criminals give for entering Saqar?
questions.answer: They were not among those who prayed, they did not feed the poor, they used to talk vainly with vain talkers, and they denied the Day of Recompense.

questions.text: Will the intercession of intercessors benefit the criminals?
questions.answer: No.

questions.text: To what are the disbelievers compared as they flee from the reminder?
questions.answer: Frightened donkeys fleeing from a lion.

questions.text: What does this Surah say some disbelievers want to be given personally?
questions.answer: Pages (of scripture) spread open specifically for him.

questions.text: Is the Quran a reminder for everyone?
questions.answer: Yes, but only those will remember whom Allah wills.

questions.text: How is Allah described at the end of the Surah?
questions.answer: As the Worthy of fear and the Worthy of forgiveness (Ahlut-Taqwa wa Ahlul-Maghfirah).

questions.text: What warning does the Surah give about the Day of Resurrection for disbelievers?
questions.answer: That it will be a difficult day, not easy for the disbelievers.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the choice to 'proceed or stay behind' in faith?
questions.answer: That the warning of Saqar is for whoever wills to advance in goodness or stay behind in disbelief.
